---
title: "omni-clustbench across backends+seeds+runs / Fig 2"
author: "Mark Robinson (took a couple snippets from Izaskun's original)"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: readable
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: hide
    code_download: true
    number_sections: true
    df_print: default
    highlight: tango
    keep_md: true
    self_contained: false
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---


```{r setup, message = FALSE}
library(knitr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(cowplot)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = TRUE,
  warning = TRUE,
  message = TRUE,
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 10,
  fig.path = "plots/",
  dev = c("png", "svg"),
  cache.lazy = FALSE,
  cache = TRUE)

```

## Doing updates to local (b/c interactive is easier) via:

```
rsync -avu mark@omnibenchmark.mls.uzh.ch:~/projects/omb/omnibenchmark_paper_code/results/ ./results/
```

```{r}

# Our dirs split look like this:
#[[721]]
# [1] "results"                                   "out-conda_seed-2_run-1"                   
# [3] "data"                                      "clustbench"                               
# [5] "dataset_generator-fcps_dataset_name-tetra" "clustering"                               
# [7] "genieclust"                                "method-ica"                               
# [9] "metrics"                                   "partition_metrics"                        
#[11] "metric-normalized_clustering_accuracy"     "parameters.json"

# "universal" directory name to type
dir_to_type <- function(d) {
  ss <- str_split(dirname(d), fixed("/"))
  bsr <- sapply(ss, .subset, 2) %>%
    str_replace("out_","") %>%
    str_replace("seed_","") %>%
    str_replace("run_","") %>%
    str_split_fixed("-", 3) %>%
    `colnames<-`(c("backend","seed","run"))
    
  dataset <- sapply(ss, .subset, 5) %>% 
    str_replace("dataset_generator-","") %>%
    str_replace("dataset_name-","")

  method <- paste0(sapply(ss, .subset, 7), "_",
                   sapply(ss, .subset, 8))
  method[method=="NA_NA"] <- NA
  
  metric <- sapply(ss, .subset, 11)
  
 data.frame(bsr, dataset, method, metric)
}


extractor <- function(u) {
  if(nrow(u))
      return(data.frame(value=as.numeric(u[1,3]),
                        k=colnames(u)[3]) )
  else
    return( data.frame(value=NA, k=NA) )
}


```

## Computational performance

```{r}
# params <- dir(pattern = "parameters.json", 
#               recursive = TRUE)

perf_files <- dir(pattern = "clustbench_performance.txt", 
            recursive = TRUE)
length(perf_files)

# data_files <- dir(pattern = "clustbench.data.gz", 
#             recursive = TRUE)
# data_type <- dir_to_type(data_files)


# only need look at methods-run-on-datasets performance
perf_type <- dir_to_type(perf_files)
head(perf_type, 2)
w <- with(perf_type, !is.na(method) & is.na(metric))
perf_files <- perf_files[w]
perf_type <- perf_type[w,]
length(perf_files)

# read in a ton of 1-line files
system.time({
perfs <- lapply(perf_files, read_tsv, skip = 1, 
         col_names = c("s","time","max_rss","max_vms",
                       "max_uss","max_pss","io_in","io_out",
                       "mean_load","cpu_time"),
         col_types = c("d","t",rep("?",8)),
         n_max = 1) %>% bind_rows()
}) 

perf_df <- cbind(perf_type %>% dplyr::select(-metric), perfs)

# noting high level of NAs in snakemake outputs (many processes are short, I guess)
colMeans(is.na(perf_df))

table(is.na(perf_df$max_rss), is.na(perf_df$io_in))

ggplot(perf_df, aes(x=is.na(cpu_time), y=s)) + geom_violin() + 
  scale_y_log10()

# cpu_time is a lower bound
ggplot(perf_df, aes(x=cpu_time, y=s)) + geom_point() + 
  scale_y_log10() + scale_x_log10() + geom_abline(colour="blue")

perf_df <- perf_df %>% filter(!is.na(cpu_time))

```


## Parsing out clustering performances

```{r}
score_files <- dir(pattern = "clustbench.scores.gz",
                   recursive = TRUE)

score_type <- dir_to_type(score_files)
head(score_type, 2)
nrow(score_type)

system.time({
rawscores <- lapply(score_files, read_csv, 
         col_types = rep("d",5), name_repair = "unique_quiet",
         n_max = 1)
}) 


extracted <- lapply(rawscores, extractor)

table(sapply(extracted, nrow))
nrow(score_type)

scores_df <- data.frame(score_type, extracted %>% bind_rows())

scores_df %>% filter(metric=="metric-adjusted_rand_score",
                     method=="agglomerative_linkage-average",
                     dataset=="fcps_chainlink") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-dataset, -method, -metric)

table(is.na(scores_df$value), scores_df$backend)

scores_df <- scores_df %>% filter(!is.na(value))


# > table(is.na(scores_df$value), scores_df$backend)
#        
#         conda envmodules  oras
#   FALSE 28821      28839 28851
#   TRUE    195        177   162
  
```


## Looking at runtime performances

```{r}

dl <- "/"
perf_key <- with(perf_df, paste0(backend, dl, seed, dl, 
                                 run, dl, dataset, dl, method))
score_key <- with(scores_df, paste0(backend, dl, seed, dl, 
                                 run, dl, dataset, dl, method))

length(perf_key)
length(score_key)

# high
mean(perf_key %in% score_key) 

# low; prob b/c many runs are fast and not captured
mean(score_key %in% perf_key) 

# which(!(perf_key %in% score_key))

# only key performances where a score was actually calculated
perf_df <- perf_df %>% filter(perf_key %in% score_key)
dim(perf_df)

cnd <- perf_df %>% filter(backend=="conda") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-backend)
app <- perf_df %>% filter(backend=="oras") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-backend)
lmd <- perf_df %>% filter(backend=="envmodules") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-backend)

df <- inner_join(lmd, app, by = c("seed", "dataset", "method", "run"),
                 suffix = c(".envmodules",".apptainer")) %>%
  inner_join(cnd, by = c("seed", "dataset", "method", "run"))

eps <- 1

df <- df %>% mutate(A.apptainer = log2(cpu_time.apptainer+cpu_time+eps),
                    M.apptainer = log((cpu_time.apptainer+eps)/(cpu_time+eps)),
                    A.envmod = log2(cpu_time.envmodules+cpu_time+eps),
                    M.envmod = log((cpu_time.envmodules+eps)/(cpu_time+eps)))


ggplot(df, aes(A.apptainer, M.apptainer, shape=as.factor(seed))) + 
  geom_point()

ggplot(df, aes(A.envmod, M.envmod, shape=as.factor(seed))) + 
  geom_point()

df.f <- df %>% filter(cpu_time > 45 | cpu_time.apptainer > 45 |
                        cpu_time.envmodules > 45) %>%
  mutate(lct.conda=log(cpu_time),
         lct.apptainer=log(cpu_time.apptainer),
         lct.envmodules=log(cpu_time.envmodules))


# linear models?
library(MASS)
fit <- lm(lct.conda-lct.apptainer ~ 1, data=df.f)
summary(fit)

fit <- lm(lct.conda ~ 0+lct.envmodules, data=df.f)
summary(fit)



md.app <- median(df$cpu_time.apptainer/df$cpu_time)

p <- ggplot(df, 
       aes(cpu_time, cpu_time.apptainer/cpu_time,
           shape=as.factor(seed))) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = md.app,
             colour="blue", linetype="dashed") +
  geom_point() + 
  scale_x_log10(limits = c(10, 10000)) +
  scale_y_log10(limits = c(.5, 2)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour="black") +
  xlab("time(s): conda") +
  ylab("CPU time: apptainer / conda") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position="none", 
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 18)) +
  ggtitle("omni-clustbench: apptainer vs. conda")
  
p <- p +
  annotate(geom = "curve", x = 5000, y = 1.414165, xend = 10000, 
           yend = md.app, curvature = -.2, 
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(2, "mm")),
           colour = "blue") +
  annotate(geom = "text", x = 5000, y = 1.5, label = round(md.app, 2), 
           hjust = "center", colour = "blue")


md.env <- median(df$cpu_time.envmodules/df$cpu_time)

q <- ggplot(df, 
       aes(cpu_time, cpu_time.envmodules/cpu_time,
           shape=as.factor(seed))) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = md.env,
             colour="blue", linetype="dashed") +
  geom_point() + 
  scale_x_log10(limits = c(10, 10000), 
                expand = expansion(mult = c(.05, .05))) +
  scale_y_log10(limits = c(.5, 2)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour="black") +
  xlab("time(s): conda") +
  ylab("CPU time: envmodules / conda") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position="none", 
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 18)) +
  ggtitle("omni-clustbench: envmodules vs. conda")

q <- q +
  annotate(geom = "curve", x = 5000, y = 1.414165, xend = 10000, 
           yend = md.env, curvature = -.2, 
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(2, "mm")),
           colour = "blue") +
  annotate(geom = "text", x = 5000, y = 1.5, label = round(md.env, 2), 
           hjust = "center", colour = "blue")

cowplot::plot_grid(p,q)

# ggplot(df %>% filter(cpu_time > 45 | cpu_time.envmodules > 45), 
#        aes(cpu_time, cpu_time.envmodules, shape=as.factor(seed),
#            colour = mean_load-mean_load.envmodules)) + 
#   geom_point() + 
#   scale_x_log10() +
#   scale_y_log10() +
#   geom_abline() #+ facet_wrap(~dataset)
# 
# 
# df %>% filter(cpu_time > 40 & cpu_time.envmodules < 5)


# bunch of random plots

# ggplot(df, aes(is.na(max_pss), s)) + geom_boxplot() + geom_jitter() +
#   scale_y_log10()
# 
# ggplot(df, aes(s, cpu_time)) + geom_point() +
#   scale_y_log10() + scale_x_log10()
#   
# 
# ggplot(df, aes(s, max_rss)) +
#   geom_point() + scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10()
# 
# ggplot(df, aes(s, io_in)) +
#   geom_point() + scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10()
# 
# ggplot(df, aes(cpu_time, cpu_time.envmodules)) + 
#   geom_point() + 
#   scale_x_log10() +
#   scale_y_log10() +
#   geom_abline() +
#   facet_wrap(~dataset)

```


## Looking at adjusted rand indices

```{r}

scores_df %>% filter(metric=="metric-adjusted_rand_score",
                     method=="agglomerative_linkage-average",
                     dataset=="fcps_chainlink") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-dataset, -method, -metric)


cnd <- scores_df %>% filter(backend=="conda", 
                            metric=="metric-adjusted_rand_score") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-backend, -metric)
app <- scores_df %>% filter(backend=="oras", 
                            metric=="metric-adjusted_rand_score") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-backend, -metric)
lmd <- scores_df %>% filter(backend=="envmodules", 
                            metric=="metric-adjusted_rand_score") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-backend, -metric)


df <- inner_join(lmd, app, by = c("seed", "dataset", "method", "run"),
                 suffix = c(".envmodules",".apptainer")) %>%
  inner_join(cnd, by = c("seed", "dataset", "method", "run"))


table(abs(df$value-df$value.apptainer))
table(abs(df$value-df$value.envmodules))

p <- ggplot(df, aes(value, value.apptainer)) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_abline(colour="black") +
  xlab("ARI: conda") +
  ylab("ARI: apptainer") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position="none", 
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 18)) +
  ggtitle("omni-clustbench: apptainer vs. conda")

q <- ggplot(df, aes(value, value.envmodules)) + 
  geom_point() +
  geom_abline(colour="black") +
  xlab("ARI: conda") +
  ylab("ARI: envmodules") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position="none", 
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 18)) +
  ggtitle("omni-clustbench: envmodules vs. conda")

q <- q +
  annotate(geom = "curve", x = .75, y = .5, xend = .29, 
           yend = .1443, curvature = -.2, 
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(2, "mm")),
           colour = "blue") +
  annotate(geom = "text", x = .75, y = .54, label = "Ward (wut/z1)", 
           hjust = "center", colour = "blue")

cowplot::plot_grid(p,q)

  
# ggplot(df, aes(value, value.envmodules)) + 
#   geom_point()

df %>% dplyr::filter(abs(value-value.envmodules)>0.001 |
                       abs(value-value.apptainer)>0.001) %>%
  dplyr::select(-k.apptainer,-k.envmodules)
df %>% dplyr::filter(abs(value-value.envmodules)>0.1 |
                       abs(value-value.apptainer)>0.1) %>%
  dplyr::select(-k.apptainer,-k.envmodules)


# there is one case where they are different
df <- inner_join(lmd %>% dplyr::filter(run == "1"), 
                 app %>% dplyr::filter(run == "1"), 
                 by = c("dataset", "method", "run"),
                 suffix = c(".envmodules",".apptainer"),
                 relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  inner_join(cnd %>% dplyr::filter(run == "1"), 
             by = c("dataset", "method", "run"),
             relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  dplyr::select(-run, -k.apptainer, -k.envmodules) %>%
  dplyr::filter(seed.envmodules != seed,
                seed.apptainer != seed)

df %>% filter(abs(value-value.envmodules)>0.0000001)



```
